articles.full_text:
UP braces for severe heatwave:Temperature nears 45°C in Banda, alert in 22 districts
- Copy link
As storms and rain subsided across Uttar Pradesh, intense heat returned to the state, with temperatures nearing 45°C. The sky remained clear in most cities from Sunday morning, while bright sunshine and low wind speed increased humidity levels.
The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for 22 cities today. Rising daytime temperatures are expected to intensify the heat, with temperatures likely to continue increasing over the next five days. From May 20 onwards, the heatwave is expected to become more severe, affecting more than half of the state’s cities.
No rainfall was recorded anywhere in the state during the past 24 hours. Banda remained the hottest city for the fifth consecutive day, recording a maximum temperature of 44.8°C. Azamgarh was the coolest city, where the minimum temperature settled at 20.2°C.
Lucknow meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh said-
The monsoon is expected to reach Kerala around May 26. Based on current estimates, it may enter Uttar Pradesh through Gorakhpur around June 18. However, this is only a probable date. The exact timeline will be assessed after the monsoon advances from Kerala. At present, there is no immediate relief from the heat. The entire state will remain under severe heat conditions for at least the next week.
See photos-
Weather forecast for the next 5 days
May 18- Strong sunshine is expected across East and West UP. Heatwave conditions may prevail during the afternoon, while winds could blow at speeds of 20–30 kmph.
May 19- Light cloud cover may appear in some areas, but no rainfall is expected. Heatwave conditions are likely during the afternoon.
May 20- The weather will remain dry in West UP, with no storm or rain alert. Some areas of East UP may receive light rainfall.
May 21- Light showers may occur in parts of north-eastern Purvanchal, while severe heat will continue across West UP.
May 22- The weather is expected to remain clear in West UP, where heatwave conditions will persist. Some eastern districts may receive light rain.
Weather conditions in major cities
Lucknow
Bright sunshine and clear skies have prevailed since morning, accompanied by hot winds. The maximum temperature is expected to reach 41°C, while the minimum may remain around 27°C. Intense heat is also likely during the night.
Kanpur
Strong sunshine has continued since morning, and a heatwave alert has been issued for the afternoon. People stepping outdoors were seen covering their faces with cloth, gamchas and angocchas. No relief from the heat is expected for at least a week.
Gorakhpur
Temperatures are rising due to strong sunshine and westerly winds. Roads witnessed thinner crowds during the day. Intense sunshine is expected to trouble residents, while humid conditions may continue at night.
Meerut
The weather has remained clear since morning with bright sunshine. For the first time this May, the temperature may cross 40°C. Morning temperatures were already recorded above 30°C, while wind speeds could remain around 10–12 kmph.
Ghaziabad
Bright sunshine has prevailed since morning, with severe heat expected during the day. The temperature was recorded at 29°C in the early morning, while the maximum may touch 40.8°C later in the day. Wind speed is expected to remain around 10 kmph.
UP likely to receive below-normal monsoon rainfall
The Meteorological Department has predicted below-normal rainfall in Uttar Pradesh between June and September.
India Meteorological Department Director General Dr M. Mohapatra said weather conditions resembling La Niña in the Pacific Ocean are now showing signs of shifting towards El Niño conditions, which could reduce rainfall. This indicates that the southwest monsoon may remain weaker than normal this year.
He added that low snow cover in the Northern Hemisphere between January and March could also affect the monsoon.
Last year’s monsoon was 10–15% above normal
According to the Meteorological Department, Uttar Pradesh received an average rainfall of 870–900 mm during the 2025 monsoon season between June and September. This was around 10–15% higher than the normal monsoon average.
Crops damaged by storms and rain; Yogi assures support
Heavy rain and storms on Wednesday and Thursday caused widespread damage to crops across the state. Vegetable crops such as cucumber, gherkin and watermelon were among the worst affected.
Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath said on Thursday that the government stands with farmers and the public during this difficult period. He said authorities are continuously monitoring the situation to ensure timely assistance reaches those in need, and warned that negligence by officials during relief operations would not be tolerated.
Why is the weather fluctuating?
According to the Meteorological Department, weather conditions in the hills of Uttarakhand have been changing continuously, with snowfall in some areas and rainfall in others. On certain days, the weather clears with strong sunshine.
Similar fluctuations are expected to continue over the next few days, affecting Uttar Pradesh as well. Winds blowing from the hills have helped keep temperatures comparatively lower at times and are also creating conditions favourable for rainfall.
